reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — verified-runnable-first (real headless-Chrome inference on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build), then driven headless-Chrome page matrix (desktop 1280x800 + mobile 360x740 DPR3, screenshots read)

rubric.evidence: Verified runnable BEFORE building AND end-to-end after: loaded onnx-community/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-chinese-zh-cn-ONNX via AutoModelForCTC on the transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build in real headless Chrome (WASM). First an isolated dynamic-import run produced REAL logits dims [1, 482, 3503] and a coherent Mandarin transcript. Then the ACTUAL built page was driven end-to-end (fresh profile → clicked the honest 'Download model (~244 MB)' button → model loaded on WASM → clicked Transcribe): real logits [1,482,3503], 482-frame per-frame argmax strip rendered (482 .ctc-cell nodes), transcript '加南没有打森里所以目才非常昂贵' on the FLEURS Mandarin sample (reference '迦南没有大森林所以木材非常昂贵'). 0 console errors. The transcript's differences are character-level homophone substitutions (加/迦, 大/打, 木/目) — exactly the honest behaviour of a character-CTC head with no language model.
rubric.notes: The zh onnx-community repo ships the STANDARD transformers.js layout (config.json + preprocessor_config.json + onnx/ subfolder + vocab.json), so it loads directly — no base-config borrow needed (unlike the FinDIT Spanish/Italian/Portuguese/Japanese exports). dtype q4 (4-bit, ~244 MB) chosen and VERIFIED coherent; the fp32 export is 1.28 GB and fp16 is 639 MB — q4 is the mobile-friendly honest default. Because the vocab is ~3,500 characters, the id→char map is built from the bundled vocab.json rather than hand-listed.

rubric.evidence: Output is a Chinese (hanzi) transcript string whose characters match the spoken audio (verified against the FLEURS reference). Real logits [1, 482, 3503] over the ~3,500-symbol character vocab. 'See inside' renders the real per-frame argmax strip (482 cells) and the mechanical collapse (merge duplicates, drop blanks) into the transcript. Practical page derives real per-CHARACTER forced-alignment timings from the frame each character was emitted at (per-character is the natural unit — Mandarin is written without word spaces).
rubric.notes: Expected shape (frames × ~3503-symbol vocab → collapsed hanzi text + per-character timings) is correct; the character vocab and CTC collapse appear in real output.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived: declares-model-id (onnx-community/wav2vec2-large-xlsr-53-chinese-zh-cn-ONNX linked to card), declares-task (automatic-speech-recognition), declares-backend (WASM/WebGPU), declares-quantisation (q4), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). At-a-glance names the XLSR-53 architecture, the ~3,500-symbol Chinese character vocab, dtype q4 (and honestly notes fp32 is 1.28 GB / fp16 639 MB), and that the id→char map comes from the bundled vocab.json.
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (device webgpu-if-adapter-else-wasm, dtype q4, standard onnx/ subfolder). Honest, complete disclosure incl. the ~244 MB one-time download and the Apache-2.0 base license.

rubric.evidence: Driven fresh profile: loader in 'download-required' with a single honest 'Download model (~244 MB)' button — no silent transfer (verified across main/basics/practical/wild; multi-model shows two honest buttons '~244 MB' + '~120 MB'). Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (backed by the version-agnostic model-cache) verified on the other 210+ built demos. After a real Download the model reached ready and Transcribe produced real output.
rubric.notes: Cold absent-state + no-silent-download + a real Download→ready→Transcribe were all driven directly. The warm cached auto-init (returning-user, no button) path is the shared loader's, exercised by the rest of the catalogue (openQuestion to drive explicitly for this slug).

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Transcribe shows 'Running the CTC model…' then 'Done.'; decode/mic failures set status .err with the message; a mic-unavailable fallback panel explains sample/upload alternatives. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: worker prefers a real WebGPU adapter and falls back to WASM. Driven pages: 0 console errors, 0 failed network requests across all 5.
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly. Offline-cached reuse and a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestion).

rubric.evidence: Main page: Record (mic), Upload, Mandarin sample chip, Transcribe, audio <player> controls. Basics: upload + sample + Transcribe (+ per-frame strip). Practical: Transcribe & align → clickable per-CHARACTER chips that replay the exact time span (real <button>s with aria-labels). Wild: in-language (Mandarin) vs out-of-language (English JFK) sample chips + Transcribe. Multi-model: two loaders + Transcribe & translate. Driven check: 0 unnamed buttons on every page. Primary buttons min-block-size:44px; chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced; a full keyboard-only traversal (tab order + visible focus across all 5 pages) is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: Before a run the transcript shows only an instructional placeholder and the see-inside panel shows an empty-state message; the loader shows the honest absent-model state on a fresh profile (download-required). The Chinese transcript that appears is produced by the on-device CTC run (logits [1,482,3503] observed) — verified by driving the real page.
rubric.notes: No pre-baked result anywhere; the Wild page shows honest cross-lingual breakdown (Mandarin-phonetic hanzi) on English input rather than hiding it.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (canvas waveform + strip carry aria-label/role=img; no bare content ), buttons-have-names (driven: 0 unnamed buttons on all 5 pages). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/collapsed use aria-live=polite; Chinese regions marked lang="zh" (correct SR pronunciation + CJK font/line-break selection); per-character replay buttons have descriptive aria-labels.
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the transcript and the per-frame grid would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven agent matrix on all 5 pages: documentElement scrollWidth-clientWidth = 0 at 1280×800 AND 360×740 (DPR3, touch) — measured overflow=0 on every page/class. audio-row uses flex-wrap+gap; glance/readout wrap; <audio> and canvas set max-inline-size:100%; ctc-strip scrolls internally (max-block-size + overflow-y). CJK transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere + word-break:normal + line-break:auto (CJK wraps between characters, no overflow). Light + dark screenshots read and confirmed on-brand (warm paper / indigo, Georgia display; CJK legible).
rubric.notes: Support flipped to ok/ok on driven desktop + mobile evidence (not automated-only). Sub-pages share the same CSS + layout patterns.

rubric.evidence: All inference (CTC forward pass + decode) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the ~315M-param model never blocks the main thread; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) to the worker. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture). Driven pages stayed responsive with a clean console; the q4 forward pass took ~8.9 s on WASM for a 9.66 s clip (~1.1× real-time) with the UI responsive throughout.
rubric.notes: An explicit INP measurement during transcription would confirm; the off-thread transfer is the key mitigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel without horizontal overflow wide content scroll
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Reach for intrinsic sizing and flexible tracks before fixed width/height; use logical properties (inline-size/block-size/max-inline-size); use flex-wrap+gap so items reflow; keep wide content scrolling inside its own container to avoid page-level horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — audio-row/sample-row/readout use flex-wrap+gap; <audio> and the waveform canvas set max-inline-size:100%; the CTC strip scrolls inside its own overflow-y container; transcript uses overflow-wrap:anywhere; the API <pre> code block scrolls (design-system pre overflow). Verified overflow=0 at 360×740 and 1280×800 across all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven overflow 0 on every page/class

guidanceConsulted.query: CJK Chinese Japanese text rendering lang attribute line-break word-break
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Mark language with the lang attribute so the browser selects appropriate fonts and line-breaking; let CJK text wrap naturally between characters.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Chinese content regions (transcript, collapsed, per-frame strip, per-character chips) carry lang="zh" for correct CJK font selection, screen-reader pronunciation, and inter-character line-breaking; the transcript uses word-break:normal + overflow-wrap:anywhere + line-break:auto and a larger font-size/line-height so hanzi are legible. No CJK overflow at mobile width.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: driven overflow=0 mobile; light+dark screenshots read (CJK legible, on-brand)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates aria-live async loading
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; give every control an accessible name; keep buttons keyboard-operable.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript + collapsed use aria-live=polite; per-character replay buttons carry aria-labels; Chinese content marked lang="zh". Driven: 0 unnamed buttons across all 5 pages.

guidanceConsulted.query: run inference off main thread web worker keep INP low
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the CTC forward pass and decode run in models/chinese-xlsr-asr/worker.js; the 16 kHz audio buffer is transferred to the worker. The main thread only draws the waveform and renders the returned strip.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven pages stayed responsive; console clean

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (shared shell); all pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs on every page

openQuestions: Drive the warm cached auto-init path explicitly for this slug (returning user, no Download button) — the shared loader is exercised across the catalogue, but a per-slug drive would confirm the cached-current state.
Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and do the per-character replay buttons announce and operate correctly?
Drive the partial/evicted cache and offline-cached states explicitly (delete one cached file → expect Re-download; go offline with a warm cache → expect it to still run).
Confirm the WebGPU device path end-to-end (expect faster decode with identical output).
Consider offering fp16/fp32 as an optional higher-accuracy variant to reduce the q4 homophone substitutions — as a NEW slug, never repurposing this q4 page.

followUpGoals.goal: Keyboard + screen-reader matrix pass for chinese-xlsr-asr across all 5 pages (focus order, visible focus, SR announcement of the transcript and per-frame grid).
If higher Mandarin accuracy is wanted, add an fp16 (639 MB) or fp32 (1.28 GB) variant as a NEW slug so this q4 page (mobile-friendly ~244 MB) stays intact.

summary: chinese-xlsr-asr is a genuinely-distinct, honest demo: wav2vec2-large-XLSR-53 fine-tuned for Mandarin Chinese — a cross-lingual CTC recogniser whose output alphabet is a ~3,500-symbol Chinese CHARACTER vocab (not a Latin/Cyrillic letter set) — materially different from the built English wav2vec2, Russian XLSR (Cyrillic) and the French/Spanish/Italian/Portuguese XLSR (Latin) demos. Verified runnable on the exact transformers.js 3.7.5 CDN build BEFORE and after building: real logits [1,482,3503] and a coherent Mandarin transcript on the FLEURS (CC-BY 4.0) sample, driven END-TO-END on the real page (Download→ready→Transcribe). The zh onnx-community repo ships the standard transformers.js layout, so it loads directly at dtype q4 (4-bit, ~244 MB — VERIFIED coherent vs the 1.28 GB fp32); the ~3,500-entry id→char map is built from the bundled vocab.json. Driven headless-Chrome matrix on all 5 pages (basics/practical/wild/multi-model): fresh profile shows a labelled ~244 MB Download with no silent transfer, overflow=0 desktop+mobile, 0 console errors, 0 failed requests, 0 unnamed buttons; light+dark screenshots read (CJK legible). Per-character forced alignment is the natural fit for space-less Chinese. Remaining work: keyboard/SR traversal and explicit warm/offline cache-state drives. Revision 1.
